result.name: Apache Tomcat End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=The Apache Tomcat version on the remote host has reached the end of life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.|insight=|affected=|impact=An EOL version of Apache Tomcat is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unfixed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.|solution=Update the Apache Tomcat version on the remote host to a still supported version.|vuldetect=Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update the Apache Tomcat version on the remote host to a still supported version.

result.name: Apache Tomcat 'CORS Filter' Setting Security Bypass Vulnerability

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat 'CORS Filter' Setting Security Bypass Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a security bypass vulnerability.|insight=The flaw exists because defaults settings for the CORS filter provided in Apache Tomcat are insecure and enable 'supportsCredentials' for all origins.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.8 Apache Tomcat versions 8.5.0 to 8.5.31 Apache Tomcat versions 8.0.0.RC1 to 8.0.52 Apache Tomcat versions 7.0.41 to 7.0.88|impact=Successful exploitation will allow remote attackers to bypass certain security restrictions and perform unauthorized actions. This may aid in further attacks.|solution=Upgrade to Apache Tomcat version 9.0.9, 8.0.53, 7.0.89 or 8.5.32 or later. Please see the references for more information.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to Apache Tomcat version 9.0.9, 8.0.53, 7.0.89 or 8.5.32 or later. Please see the references for more information.

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 8.5.32 Installation path / port: 8040/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat RCE Vulnerability (Mar 2025) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat RCE Vulnerability (Mar 2025) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability.|insight=The original implementation of partial PUT used a temporary file based on the user provided file name and path with the path separator replaced by '.'.
If all of the following are true, a malicious user is able to view security sensitive files and/or inject content into those files:
- writes enabled for the default servlet (disabled by default)
- support for partial PUT (enabled by default)
- a target URL for security sensitive uploads that is a sub-directory of a target URL for public uploads
- attacker knowledge of the names of security sensitive files being uploaded
- the security sensitive files also being uploaded via partial PUT
If all of the following are true, a malicious user is able to perform remote code execution:
- writes enabled for the default servlet (disabled by default)
- support for partial PUT (enabled by default)
- application is using Tomcat's file based session persistence with the default storage location
- application includes a library that may be leveraged in a deserialization attack|affected=Apache Tomcat version 9.0.98 and prior, 10.x through 10.1.34 and 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.2.
Note: While not explicitly mentioned by the vendor (due to the EOL status of these branches) it is assumed that the whole 10.x branch and all versions prior to 9.x are affected by these flaws. If you disagree with this assessment and want to accept the risk please create an override for this result.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.0.99, 10.1.35, 11.0.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 9.0.99 Installation path / port: 8040/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat Rewrite Rule Bypass Vulnerability (Apr 2025) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Rewrite Rule Bypass Vulnerability (Apr 2025) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a rewrite rule bypass vulnerability.|insight=For a subset of unlikely rewrite rule configurations, it is possible for a specially crafted request to bypass some rewrite rules. If those rewrite rules effectively enforced security constraints, those constraints could be bypassed.|affected=Apache Tomcat version 9.0.102 and prior, 10.x through 10.1.39 and 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.5.
Note: While not explicitly mentioned by the vendor (due to the EOL status of these branches) it is assumed that the whole 10.x branch and all versions prior to 9.x are affected by these flaws. result.name: Apache Tomcat AJP RCE Vulnerability (Ghostcat) - Active Check

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat AJP RCE Vulnerability (Ghostcat) - Active Check
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in the AJP connector dubbed 'Ghostcat'.|insight=Apache Tomcat server has a file containing vulnerability, which can be used by an attacker to read or include any files in all webapp directories on Tomcat, such as webapp configuration files or source code.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions prior 7.0.100, 8.5.51 or 9.0.31 when the AJP connector is enabled.
Other products like JBoss or Wildfly which are using Tomcat might be affected as well.|impact=|solution=- Update Apache Tomcat to version 7.0.100, 8.5.51, 9.0.31 or later
- For other products using Tomcat please contact the vendor for more information on fixed versions|vuldetect=Sends a crafted AJP request and checks the response.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution:
- Update Apache Tomcat to version 7.0.100, 8.5.51, 9.0.31 or later
- For other products using Tomcat please contact the vendor for more information on fixed versions

result.description: The returned status is '500', which should be '403' on a patched system, when trying to read a file which indicates that the installation is vulnerable.

result.name: Apache Tomcat Request Mix-up Vulnerability (May 2022) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Request Mix-up Vulnerability (May 2022) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:L|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a request mix-up vulnerability.|insight=If a web application sends a WebSocket message concurrently with the WebSocket connection closing, it is possible that the application will continue to use the socket after it has been closed. The error handling triggered in this case could cause the a pooled object to be placed in the pool twice. This could result in subsequent connections using the same object concurrently which could result in data being returned to the wrong use and/or other errors.|affected=Apache Tomcat 8.5.0 through 8.5.75 and 9.0.0.M1 through 9.0.20.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.76, 9.0.21 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 8.5.76 Installation path / port: 8081/tcp

The error handling triggered in this case could cause the a pooled object to be placed in the pool twice. This could result in subsequent connections using the same object concurrently which could result in data being returned to the wrong use and/or other errors.|affected=Apache Tomcat 8.5.0 through 8.5.75 and 9.0.0.M1 through 9.0.20.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.76, 9.0.21 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Tomcat HTTP/2 Protocol DoS Vulnerability (MadeYouReset) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat HTTP/2 Protocol DoS Vulnerability (MadeYouReset) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability in the HTTP/2 protocol dubbed 'MadeYouReset'.|insight=A mismatch caused by client-triggered server-sent stream resets between HTTP/2 specifications and the internal architectures of some HTTP/2 implementations may result in excessive server resource consumption leading to denial-of-service (DoS). By opening streams and then rapidly triggering the server to reset them, using malformed frames or flow control errors, an attacker can exploit incorrect stream accounting. Streams reset by the server are considered closed at the protocol level, even though backend processing continues. This allows a client to cause the server to handle an unbounded number of concurrent streams on a single connection.|affected=Apache Tomcat version 9.0.107 and prior, 10.x through 10.1.43 and 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.9.
Note: While not explicitly mentioned by the vendor (due to the EOL status of these branches) it is assumed that the whole 10.x branch and all versions prior to 9.x are affected by these flaws. If you disagree with this assessment and want to accept the risk please create an override for this result.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.0.108, 10.1.44, 11.0.10 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 9.0.108 Installation path / port: 8081/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat Session Fixation Vulnerability (Aug 2025) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Session Fixation Vulnerability (Aug 2025) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a session fixation vulnerability.|insight=If the rewrite valve was enabled for a web application, an attacker was able to craft a URL that, if a victim clicked on it, would cause the victim's interaction with that resource to occur in the context of the attacker's session.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions prior to 9.0.106, 10.1.0-M1 through 10.1.41 and 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.7.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.0.106, 10.1.42, 11.0.8 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 9.0.106 Installation path / port: 8040/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat DoS Vulnerability (Jul 2024) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat DoS Vulnerability (Jul 2024) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=When processing an HTTP/2 stream, Tomcat did not handle some cases of excessive HTTP headers correctly. This led to a miscounting of active HTTP/2 streams which in turn led to the use of an incorrect infinite timeout which allowed connections to remain open which should have been closed.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions prior to 9.0.90, 10.x through 10.1.24 and 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.0-M20.
Note: While not explicitly mentioned by the vendor (due to the EOL status of these branches) it is assumed that the whole 10.x branch and all versions prior to 9.x are affected by this flaw. If you disagree with this assessment and want to accept the risk please create an override for this result.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.0.90, 10.1.25, 11.0.0-M21 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 9.0.90 Installation path / port: 8081/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat DoS Vulnerability (Jul 2025) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat DoS Vulnerability (Jul 2025) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=A race condition on connection close could trigger a JVM crash when using the APR/Native connector leading to a DoS. This was particularly noticeable with client initiated closes of HTTP/2 connections.|affected=Apache Tomcat version 9.0.106 and prior.
Note: While not explicitly mentioned by the vendor (due to the EOL status of these branches) it is assumed that all versions prior to 9.x are affected by these flaws. If you disagree with this assessment and want to accept the risk please create an override for this result.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.0.107 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

Note: While not explicitly mentioned by the vendor (due to the EOL status of these branches) it is assumed that the whole 10.x branch and all versions prior to 9.x are affected by these flaws. If you disagree with this assessment and want to accept the risk please create an override for this result.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.0.106, 10.1.42, 11.0.8 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Tomcat Multiple Vulnerabilities (Oct 2023) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Multiple Vulnerabilities (Oct 2023) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2023-42795: When recycling various internal objects, including the request and the response, prior to re-use by the next request/response, an error could cause Tomcat to skip some parts of the recycling process leading to information leaking from the current request/response to the next.
- CVE-2023-44487: HTTP/2 rapid reset attack
- CVE-2023-45648: A specially crafted, invalid trailer header could cause Tomcat to treat a single request as multiple requests leading to the possibility of request smuggling when behind a reverse proxy.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions 8.5.0 through 8.5.93, 9.0.0-M1 through 9.0.80, 10.0.0 through 10.1.13 and 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.0-M11.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.94, 9.0.81, 10.1.14, 11.0.0-M12 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 8.5.94 Installation path / port: 8040/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat HTTP/2 Vulnerability (Dec 2020) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat HTTP/2 Vulnerability (Dec 2020) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability in HTTP/2.|insight=It was discovered that Apache Tomcat could re-use an HTTP request header value from the previous stream received on an HTTP/2 connection for the request associated with the subsequent stream. While this would most likely lead to an error and the closure of the HTTP/2 connection, it is possible that information could leak between requests.|affected=Apache Tomcat 8.5.0 to 8.5.59, 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.39 and 10.0.0-M1 to 10.0.0-M9.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.60, 9.0.40, 10.0.0-M10 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.|insight=When responding to new h2c connection requests, Apache Tomcat could duplicate request headers and a limited amount of request body from one request to another meaning user A and user B could both see the results of user A's request.|affected=Apache Tomcat 8.5.x - 8.5.61, 9.0.0.M1 - 9.0.41 and 10.0.x prior to 10.0.1.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.63, 9.0.43, 10.0.2 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a session fixation vulnerability.|insight=When using FORM authentication there was a narrow window where an attacker could perform a session fixation attack. The window was considered too narrow for an exploit to be practical but, erring on the side of caution, this issue has been treated as a security vulnerability.|affected=Apache Tomcat 7.0.0 to 7.0.98, 8.5.0 to 8.5.49 and 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.29.|impact=|solution=Update to version 7.0.99, 8.5.50, 9.0.30 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Tomcat DoS Vulnerability (Jun 2019) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat DoS Vulnerability (Jun 2019) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a denial of service vulnerability in the HTTP/2 implementation.|insight=The HTTP/2 implementation accepts streams with excessive numbers of SETTINGS frames and also permitts clients to keep streams open without reading/writing request/response data. By keeping streams open for requests that utilises the Servlet API's blocking I/O, clients are able to cause server-side threads to block eventually leading to thread exhaustion and a DoS.|affected=Apache Tomcat 8.5.0 to 8.5.40 and 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.19.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.41, 9.0.20 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a denial of service vulnerability.|insight=The fix for CVE-2019-0199 was incomplete and did not address HTTP/2 connection window exhaustion on write. By not sending WINDOW_UPDATE messages for the connection window (stream 0) clients are able to cause server-side threads to block eventually leading to thread exhaustion and a DoS.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions 8.5.0 to 8.5.40 and 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.19.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.41, 9.0.20 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Tomcat Request Smuggling Vulnerability (Oct 2022) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Request Smuggling Vulnerability (Oct 2022) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a request smuggling vulnerability.|insight=If Tomcat is configured to ignore invalid HTTP headers via setting rejectIllegalHeader to false (the default for 8.5.x only), Tomcat does not reject a request containing an invalid Content-Length header making a request smuggling attack possible if Tomcat is located behind a reverse proxy that also fails to reject the request with the invalid header.|affected=Apache Tomcat version 8.5.0 through 8.5.82, 9.0.0-M1 through 9.0.67, 10.0.0-M1 through 10.0.26 and 10.1.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.83, 9.0.68, 10.0.27, 10.1.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Tomcat 'Hostname Verification' Security Bypass Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat 'Hostname Verification' Security Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a security bypass vulnerability.|insight=The flaw exists due to a missing host name verification when using TLS with the WebSocket client.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.9, 8.5.0 to 8.5.31, 8.0.0.RC1 to 8.0.52 and 7.0.35 to 7.0.88 on Linux.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow an attacker to bypass certain security restrictions and perform unauthorized actions.|solution=Upgrade to Apache Tomcat version 9.0.10 or 8.5.32 or 8.0.53 or 7.0.90 or later. Please see the references for more information.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to Apache Tomcat version 9.0.10 or 8.5.32 or 8.0.53 or 7.0.90 or later. Please see the references for more information.

Please see the references for more information.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Tomcat Request Smuggling Vulnerability (Oct 2022) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Request Smuggling Vulnerability (Oct 2022) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a request smuggling vulnerability.|insight=If Tomcat is configured to ignore invalid HTTP headers via setting rejectIllegalHeader to false (the default for 8.5.x only), Tomcat does not reject a request containing an invalid Content-Length header making a request smuggling attack possible if Tomcat is located behind a reverse proxy that also fails to reject the request with the invalid header.|affected=Apache Tomcat version 8.5.0 through 8.5.82, 9.0.0-M1 through 9.0.67, 10.0.0-M1 through 10.0.26 and 10.1.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.83, 9.0.68, 10.0.27, 10.1.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 8.5.83 Installation path / port: 8081/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat Request Smuggling Vulnerability (Nov 2023) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Request Smuggling Vulnerability (Nov 2023) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a request smuggling vulnerability.|insight=Tomcat does not correctly parse HTTP trailer headers. A specially crafted trailer header that exceeds the header size limit could cause Tomcat to treat a single request as multiple requests leading to the possibility of request smuggling when behind a reverse proxy.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions 8.5.0 through 8.5.95, 9.0.0-M1 through 9.0.82, 10.1.0-M1 through 10.1.15 and 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.0-M10.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.96, 9.0.83, 10.1.16, 11.0.0-M11 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Tomcat Clustering DoS Vulnerability (May 2022)

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Clustering DoS Vulnerability (May 2022)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability when running with enabled Tomcat clustering over an untrusted network.|insight=The documentation for the EncryptInterceptor incorrectly stated it enabled Tomcat clustering to run over an untrusted network. This was not correct. While the EncryptInterceptor does provide confidentiality and integrity protection, it does not protect against all risks associated with running over any untrusted network, particularly DoS risks.|affected=- The documentation in Apache Tomcat 8.5.38 through 8.5.78, 9.0.13 through 9.0.62, 10.0.0-M1 through 10.0.20 and 10.1.0-M1 to 10.1.0-M14
- Apache Tomcat when running with enabled Tomcat clustering over an untrusted network|impact=|solution=- Don't run Apache Tomcat with enabled Tomcat clustering over an untrusted network
- Update Apache Tomcat to version 10.1.0-M15, 10.0.21, 9.0.63, 8.5.79 or later to receive the corrected / updated documentation|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
- Don't run Apache Tomcat with enabled Tomcat clustering over an untrusted network
- Update Apache Tomcat to version 10.1.0-M15, 10.0.21, 9.0.63, 8.5.79 or later to receive the corrected / updated documentation

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: See solution information Installation path / port: 8081/tcp

This was not correct. result.name: Apache Tomcat DoS Vulnerability (Feb 2023) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat DoS Vulnerability (Feb 2023) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=Apache Tomcat uses a packaged renamed copy of Apache Commons FileUpload to provide the file upload functionality defined in the Jakarta Servlet specification. Apache Tomcat was, therefore, also vulnerable to the Apache Commons FileUpload vulnerability CVE-2023-24998 as there was no limit to the number of request parts processed. This resulted in the possibility of an attacker triggering a DoS with a malicious upload or series of uploads.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions through 8.5.84, 9.0.0-M1 through 9.0.70, 10.x through 10.1.4 and 11.0.0-M1 only.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.85, 9.0.71, 10.1.5, 11.0.0-M3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 8.5.85 Installation path / port: 8081/tcp

Apache Tomcat was, therefore, also vulnerable to the Apache Commons FileUpload vulnerability CVE-2023-24998 as there was no limit to the number of request parts processed. This resulted in the possibility of an attacker triggering a DoS with a malicious upload or series of uploads.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions through 8.5.84, 9.0.0-M1 through 9.0.70, 10.x through 10.1.4 and 11.0.0-M1 only.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.85, 9.0.71, 10.1.5, 11.0.0-M3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Tomcat RCE Vulnerability (May 2020) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat RCE Vulnerability (May 2020) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability.|insight=If:
- an attacker is able to control the contents and name of a file on the server and
- the server is configured to use the PersistenceManager with a FileStore and
- the PersistenceManager is configured with sessionAttributeValueClassNameFilter='null' (the default unless a SecurityManager is used) or a sufficiently lax filter to allow the attacker provided object to be deserialized and
- the attacker knows the relative file path from the storage location used by FileStore to the file the attacker has control over
then, using a specifically crafted request, the attacker will be able to trigger remote code execution via deserialization of the file under their control. Note that all of conditions must be true for the attack to succeed.|affected=Apache Tomcat 7.0.0 to 7.0.103, 8.5.0 to 8.5.54, 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.34 and 10.0.0-M1 to 10.0.0-M4.|impact=|solution=Update to version 7.0.104, 8.5.55, 9.0.35, 10.0.0-M5 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 8.5.55 Installation path / port: 8081/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat Privilege Escalation Vulnerability (Dec 2019) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Privilege Escalation Vulnerability (Dec 2019) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a privilege escalation vulnerability.|insight=When Tomcat is configured with the JMX Remote Lifecycle Listener, a local attacker without access to the Tomcat process or configuration files is able to manipulate the RMI registry to perform a man-in-the-middle attack to capture user names and passwords used to access the JMX interface. The attacker can then use these credentials to access the JMX interface and gain complete control over the Tomcat instance.|affected=Apache Tomcat 7.0.0 to 7.0.97, 8.5.0 to 8.5.47 and 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.28.|impact=|solution=Update to version 7.0.99, 8.5.49, 9.0.29 or later. As a mitigation disable Tomcat's JmxRemoteLifecycleListener and use the built-in remote JMX facilities provided by the JVM.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 7.0.99, 8.5.49, 9.0.29 or later. As a mitigation disable Tomcat's JmxRemoteLifecycleListener and use the built-in remote JMX facilities provided by the JVM.

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 8.5.49 Installation path / port: 8081/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat RCE Vulnerability (Mar 2021) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat RCE Vulnerability (Mar 2021) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability due to an incomplete fix.|insight=The fix for CVE-2020-9484 was incomplete. When using a highly unlikely configuration edge case, the Tomcat instance is still vulnerable to CVE-2020-9484. Note that both the previously published prerequisites for CVE-2020-9484 also apply to this issue.|affected=Apache Tomcat 7.0.x - 7.0.107, 8.5.x - 8.5.61, 9.0.0.M1 - 9.0.41 and 10.0.x prior to 10.0.1.|impact=|solution=Update to version 7.0.108, 8.5.63, 9.0.43, 10.0.2 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 8.5.63 Installation path / port: 8081/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat JNDI Realm Authentication Weakness Vulnerability (Jul 2021) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat JNDI Realm Authentication Weakness Vulnerability (Jul 2021) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:H/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to an authentication weakness vulnerability in the JNDI Realm.|insight=Queries made by the JNDI Realm do not always correctly escape parameters. Parameter values could be sourced from user provided data (eg user names) as well as configuration data provided by an administrator. In limited circumstances it is possible for users to authenticate using variations of their user name and/or to bypass some of the protection provided by the LockOut Realm.|affected=Apache Tomcat 7.0.x through 7.0.108, 8.5.x through 8.5.65, 9.0.0.M1 through 9.0.45 and 10.0.0-M1 through 10.0.5.|impact=|solution=Update to version 7.0.109, 8.5.66, 9.0.46, 10.0.6 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 8.5.66 Installation path / port: 8040/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat Security Constraint Incorrect Handling Access Bypass Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Security Constraint Incorrect Handling Access Bypass Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to multiple access bypass vulnerabilities.|insight=Multiple flaws are due to:
- The system does not properly enforce security constraints that defined by annotations of Servlets in certain cases, depending on the order that Servlets are loaded.
- The URL pattern of '' (the empty string) which exactly maps to the context root was not correctly handled when used as part of a security constraint definition.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.4
Apache Tomcat versions 8.5.0 to 8.5.27
Apache Tomcat versions 8.0.0.RC1 to 8.0.49
Apache Tomcat versions 7.0.0 to 7.0.84 on Linux.|impact=Successfully exploiting these issues will allow remote attackers to bypass security constraints to access ostensibly restricted resources on the target system.|solution=Upgrade to Apache Tomcat version 9.0.5, 8.5.28, 8.0.50, 7.0.85 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to Apache Tomcat version 9.0.5, 8.5.28, 8.0.50, 7.0.85 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 8.5.28 Installation path / port: 8040/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat Authentication Bypass Vulnerability (Nov 2024) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Authentication Bypass Vulnerability (Nov 2024) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:P/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to an authentication bypass vulnerability.|insight=If Tomcat was configured to use a custom Jakarta Authentication (formerly JASPIC) ServerAuthContext component which may throw an exception during the authentication process without explicitly setting an HTTP status to indicate failure, the authentication may not have failed, allowing the user to bypass the authentication process. There are no known Jakarta Authentication components that behave in this way.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions prior to 9.0.96, 10.0.x through 10.1.30 and 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.0-M26.
Note: While not explicitly mentioned by the vendor (due to the EOL status of these branches) it is assumed that the whole 10.x branch and all versions prior to 9.x are affected by this flaw. If you disagree with this assessment and want to accept the risk please create an override for this result.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.0.96, 10.1.31, 11.0.0 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 9.0.96 Installation path / port: 8040/tcp

nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:P/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to an authentication bypass vulnerability.|insight=If Tomcat was configured to use a custom Jakarta Authentication (formerly JASPIC) ServerAuthContext component which may throw an exception during the authentication process without explicitly setting an HTTP status to indicate failure, the authentication may not have failed, allowing the user to bypass the authentication process. There are no known Jakarta Authentication components that behave in this way.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions prior to 9.0.96, 10.0.x through 10.1.30 and 11.0.0-M1 through 11.0.0-M26.
Note: While not explicitly mentioned by the vendor (due to the EOL status of these branches) it is assumed that the whole 10.x branch and all versions prior to 9.x are affected by this flaw. If you disagree with this assessment and want to accept the risk please create an override for this result.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.0.96, 10.1.31, 11.0.0 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Tomcat XSS Vulnerability (May 2019) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat XSS Vulnerability (May 2019) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to a cross-site scripting vulnerability.|insight=The SSI printenv command in Apache Tomcat echoes user provided data without escaping and is, therefore, vulnerable to XSS. SSI is disabled by default. The printenv command is intended for debugging and is unlikely to be present in a production website.|affected=Apache Tomcat versions 7.0.0 to 7.0.93, 8.5.0 to 8.5.39 and 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.17.|impact=|solution=Update to version 7.0.94, 8.5.40, 9.0.18 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Tomcat Information Disclosure Vulnerability (Jan 2021) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Information Disclosure Vulnerability (Jan 2021) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.|insight=When serving resources from a network location using the NTFS file system it was possible to bypass security constraints and/or view the source code for JSPs in some configurations. The root cause was the unexpected behaviour of the JRE API File.getCanonicalPath() which in turn was caused by the inconsistent behaviour of the Windows API (FindFirstFileW) in some circumstances.|affected=Apache Tomcat 7.0.0 to 7.0.106, 8.5.0 to 8.5.59, 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.39 and 10.0.0-M1 to 10.0.0-M9.|impact=|solution=Update to version 7.0.107, 8.5.60, 9.0.40, 10.0.0-M10 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Tomcat HTTP Request Smuggling Vulnerability (Jul 2021) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat HTTP Request Smuggling Vulnerability (Jul 2021) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to an HTTP request smuggling vulnerability.|insight=Apache Tomcat does not correctly parse the HTTP transfer-encoding request header in some circumstances leading to the possibility to request smuggling when used with a reverse proxy. Specifically: Tomcat incorrectly ignores the transfer-encoding header if the client declared it would only accept an HTTP/1.0 response. Tomcat honours the identify encoding and Tomcat does not ensure that, if present, the chunked encoding is the final encoding.|affected=Apache Tomcat 8.5.x through 8.5.66, 9.0.0.M1 through 9.0.46 and 10.0.0-M1 through 10.0.6.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.68, 9.0.48, 10.0.7 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Tomcat Multiple Vulnerabilities (Dec 2024) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat Multiple Vulnerabilities (Dec 2024) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2024-50379: Remote code execution (RCE) via write-enabled default servlet
- CVE-2024-54677: Denial of service (DoS) in examples web application
- CVE-2024-56337: RCE via write-enabled default servlet - CVE-2024-50379 mitigation was incomplete|affected=Apache Tomcat versions prior to 9.0.98, 10.x prior to 10.1.34 and 11.x prior to 11.0.2.
Note: While not explicitly mentioned by the vendor (due to the EOL status of these branches) it is assumed that the whole 10.x branch and all versions prior to 9.x are affected by these flaws. If you disagree with this assessment and want to accept the risk please create an override for this result.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.0.98, 10.1.34, 11.0.2 or later.
Vendor note: Users running Tomcat on a case insensitive file system with the default servlet write enabled (readonly initialisation parameter set to the non-default value of false) may need additional configuration to fully mitigate CVE-2024-50379 depending on which version of Java they are using with Tomcat:
- running on Java 8 or Java 11: the system property sun.io.useCanonCaches must be explicitly set to false (it defaults to true)
- running on Java 17: the system property sun.io.useCanonCaches, if set, must be set to false (it defaults to false)
- running on Java 21 onwards: no further configuration is required (the system property and the problematic cache have been removed)|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution:
Update to version 9.0.98, 10.1.34, 11.0.2 or later.
Vendor note: Users running Tomcat on a case insensitive file system with the default servlet write enabled (readonly initialisation parameter set to the non-default value of false) may need additional configuration to fully mitigate CVE-2024-50379 depending on which version of Java they are using with Tomcat:
- running on Java 8 or Java 11: the system property sun.io.useCanonCaches must be explicitly set to false (it defaults to true)
- running on Java 17: the system property sun.io.useCanonCaches, if set, must be set to false (it defaults to false)
- running on Java 21 onwards: no further configuration is required (the system property and the problematic cache have been removed)

result.description: Installed version: 8.5.23 Fixed version: 9.0.98 Installation path / port: 8081/tcp

result.name: Apache Tomcat HTTP/2 Vulnerability (Oct 2020) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache Tomcat HTTP/2 Vulnerability (Oct 2020) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability in HTTP/2.|insight=If an HTTP/2 client exceeded the agreed maximum number of concurrent streams for a connection (in violation of the HTTP/2 protocol), it is possible that a subsequent request made on that connection could contain HTTP headers - including HTTP/2 pseudo headers - from a previous request rather than the intended headers. This could lead to users seeing responses for unexpected resources.|affected=Apache Tomcat 8.5.1 to 8.5.57, 9.0.0.M5 to 9.0.37 and 10.0.0-M1 to 10.0.0-M7.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.58, 9.0.38, 10.0.0-M8 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache Tomcat is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.|insight=The simplified implementation of blocking reads and writes introduced in Tomcat 10 and back-ported to Tomcat 9.0.47 onwards exposed a long standing (but extremely hard to trigger) concurrency bug that could cause client connections to share an Http11Processor instance resulting in responses, or part responses, to be received by the wrong client.|affected=Apache Tomcat version 8.5.0 through 8.5.77, 9.0.0-M1 through 9.0.60, 10.0.0-M1 through 10.0.18 and 10.1.0-M1 through 10.1.0-M12.|impact=|solution=Update to version 8.5.78, 9.0.62, 10.0.20, 10.1.0-M14 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure

nvt.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.|insight=The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as defined by RFC1323/RFC7323.|affected=TCP implementations that implement RFC1323/RFC7323.|impact=A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.|solution=To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.|vuldetect=Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.

result.description:
It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 2914887598 Packet 2: 2914888678

result.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure

nvt.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.|insight=The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.|affected=|impact=This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.|solution=Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)|vuldetect=Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)

result.description: The following response / ICMP packet has been received: - ICMP Type: 14 - ICMP Code: 0

This information is based on the following scripts / settings:
- HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)
- No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)
- Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)
- Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)
- The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use
- The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and 'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the 'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use
If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community forum.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

Requests to this service are done via HTTP/1.1.
This service seems to be able to host PHP scripts.

The User-Agent "Mozilla/5.0 [en] (X11, U; OpenVAS-VT <version replaced>)" was used to access the remote host.
Historic /scripts and /cgi-bin are not added to the directories used for web application scanning. You can enable this again with the "Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning" option within the "Global variable settings" of the scan config in use.
The following directories were used for web application scanning:
http://artifactory5.trabalho_vulnnet:8081/ http://artifactory5.trabalho_vulnnet:8081/artifactory

nvt.name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=The script consolidates and reports various information for web application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.
This information is based on the following scripts / settings:
- HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)
- No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)
- Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)
- Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)
- The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use
- The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and 'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the 'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use
If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community forum.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
The Hostname/IP "artifactory5.trabalho_vulnnet" was used to access the remote host.
Generic web application scanning is disabled for this host via the "Enable generic web application scanning" option within the "Global variable settings" of the scan config in use.
Requests to this service are done via HTTP/1.1.
This service seems to be able to host PHP scripts.
This service seems to be able to host ASP scripts.
The User-Agent "Mozilla/5.0 [en] (X11, U; OpenVAS-VT <version replaced>)" was used to access the remote host.
Historic /scripts and /cgi-bin are not added to the directories used for web application scanning. You can enable this again with the "Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning" option within the "Global variable settings" of the scan config in use.
The following directories were used for web application scanning:
http://artifactory5.trabalho_vulnnet:8040/ http://artifactory5.trabalho_vulnnet:8040/artifactory
While this is not, in and of itself, a bug, you should manually inspect these directories to ensure that they are in compliance with company security standards
